title: Discovery of Unstable Singularities
partial_reason: 0 mathEnvs detected — computational paper, section-level analysis only

sec:main.summary: Uses PINNs to discover unstable self-similar singularities for 3D Euler and related PDEs. Computational existence proof of blowup candidates.

sec:pinn_method.summary: PINN framework for targeted PDE solution discovery: incorporates mathematical structure into neural network architecture.

sec:self_similar.summary: Self-similar ansatz reduces time-dependent PDE to steady-state profile equation. Key reduction enabling numerical search.

sec:factorization.summary: Equation factorization to build singular behavior into network architecture, improving convergence.

sec:training.summary: Multi-stage training with second-order optimizers for high-precision solutions.

sec:lambda.summary: Algorithm for identifying admissible self-similar exponents $\lambda$ that yield genuine singularities.

sec:spectrum.summary: Linearized stability analysis: compute spectrum of linearization around discovered profile to verify instability.

sec:implications.summary: Implications for rigorous blowup: the discovered solutions are candidates for computer-assisted proofs.

edges.note: Main results rely on PINN framework

computational_framework.description: PINN-based discovery of self-similar blowup profiles

singularity_validation.description: Verify discovered solutions are genuine unstable singularities

mathematical_setup.description: Self-similar ansatz and reduction to profile equation

notes: Computational paper. No formal theorems — results are numerical discoveries. Related to Hou's prior work on potential Euler blowup. Cross-paper relevance: provides candidates that could connect to Tao-style constructions.
